prompt: Commit the staged changes. I fixed a null pointer exception in the user authentication module.
expected_output: The agent should produce a commit message like `fix(auth): resolve null pointer exception in user authentication`. It must use the `fix` type since this is a bug fix. It should check `git diff --cached` and `git log` first. It must NOT include any Co-Authored-By or Signed-off-by trailer attributing an AI.

prompt: I just added a new search feature to the API. Please commit it.
expected_output: The agent should produce a commit message like `feat(api): add search endpoint` or `feat: add search feature to API`. It must use the `feat` type since this is a new feature. No AI attribution footers.

prompt: Commit these changes. I removed the deprecated v1 API endpoints and consumers will need to migrate to v2.
expected_output: The agent should recognize this as a breaking change and use `feat(api)!: remove deprecated v1 endpoints` or similar, and/or include a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer explaining the migration requirement. The BREAKING CHANGE footer must be uppercase.

prompt: Please commit. I reorganized the utility functions into separate modules but didn't change any behavior.
expected_output: The agent should use the `refactor` type since this is a code restructuring with no behavior change. Something like `refactor: extract utility functions into separate modules`. It may include a body explaining why. No AI attribution.

prompt: Commit this. I updated the README with installation instructions and added a CONTRIBUTING.md file.
expected_output: The agent should use the `docs` type since only documentation changed. Something like `docs: add installation instructions and contributing guide`. No AI attribution.

prompt: I upgraded React from 17 to 18 and updated the webpack config. Please commit.
expected_output: The agent should use the `build` type with an appropriate scope like `deps`. Something like `build(deps): upgrade React to v18 and update webpack config`. No AI attribution.
